title: Sour Russian Cassava Tagine
description: A tangy, gooey monsoon-inspired Russian dish featuring cassava and a manteca-emulsify sauce cooked in a traditional tajine clay vessel.
instructions: Preheat the tajine clay to a medium heat.
Heat olive oil in the tajine until shimmering and add diced onion, cooking until softened.
Stir in minced garlic, grated ginger, and tomato paste, sautéing until fragrant.
Add diced cassava, sliced carrots, smoked paprika, and thyme, tossing to coat the vegetables.
Pour in vegetable broth, soy sauce, and vinegar, then stir in tomato paste again for thickness.
Cover the tajine with its lid, reduce heat to low, and let the tagine simmer for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Check the cassava for tenderness; if tender, remove the lid and increase heat for 5 minutes to thicken the sauce.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt, pepper, and additional vinegar if desired.
Transfer the tagine to a serving dish and drizzle with a splash of olive oil for shine.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley or dill before serving.
